summary: todo -> doing. Picked up for investigation (user): decide whether Vast.ai is viable as a SECOND remote-engine provider alongside RunPod. Four research agents dispatched: platform state + community sentiment (is the RunPod->Vast migration real, and why), API/CLI/SDK parity vs our RunPod surface, networking + storage risk (TLS proxy, WSS, persistent volumes, data-loss), and a read-only coupling map of routes/remote*.js + runpodRemote.js. No code changes yet - this stage is investigation only.

Investigation done -> research/01-viability.md. VERDICT: viable, but EPHEMERAL-ONLY. Key finding: Vast has NO network volumes (verified against docs.vast.ai primary source, not a review site - both storage types are host-local, volumes Cannot migrate between different physical machines; the coming-soon announcement from May 2025 is absent from the storage docs entirely as of 2026-07-23). So our volume design has no Vast equivalent. BUT we already ship the shape Vast needs: the MPI-78 Any region (no volume) ephemeral Pod. Vast maps onto that path. Feared blocker CUDA driver floor is SOLVED - Vast offer search has driver_version and cuda_max_good as first-class filter fields, a direct and arguably better equivalent to allowedCudaVersions. Real risks now: (1) bandwidth billed per byte at host-set rates, unpublished - ephemeral means re-downloading 10-40GB of weights every session and this could eat the entire GPU-hour saving; (2) endpoint discovery - no proxy.runpod.net equivalent, either Cloudflare quick tunnel with a random subdomain per boot plus a chicken-and-egg discovery loop, or direct IP:port with a pinned self-signed cert which is cleaner given our backend-proxy topology; (3) host operator can read container env on unverified hosts, so injecting the API key like we do RUNPOD_API_KEY leaks it - mitigated by Vast parameter-restricted API keys. Coupling map verified by hand: 5261 lines in the remote layer, ~1380 need ZERO changes, proxy.runpod.net appears in only 4 places and only remoteEngine.js:91 is a real construction site. Effort ~3 weeks to prototype, 5-6 to RunPod-equivalent robustness, after 3 non-optional prerequisite refactors. Migration premise UNCONFIRMED - no thread found, RunPod at 120M ARR per TechCrunch 2026-01-16; the one specific push factor found is RunPods Feb 2026 doubling of idle network-volume price to 0.20/GB/mo which hits our exact design. RECOMMENDATION: run a few-hour under-5-dollar probe on one instance (bandwidth invoice, endpoint discovery, does our cu130 image boot) BEFORE any refactor.

doing -> todo (PARKED in backlog by user decision - revisit in a few weeks, not acting now). Curated findings promoted to docs/vast-ai-research/ (README = decision doc + billing myth-check + the two red flags; api-parity.md; integration-map.md), added to the docs/ knowledge map under RunPod remote engine. brief.md now points there. User red flags: ephemeral-only kills the multi-model user (5-10 models re-download every session) and per-byte bandwidth billing can eat the GPU-hour saving. Billing myth-check recorded: the Discord only-charge-during-inference claim is Vast serverless, not the instance model we would use - a running instance bills per-second same as RunPod.
